name: half_pipe_with_symmetry_plane
description: Half-cylinder pipe (radius 50 mm, length 300 mm) lying along +z, half-disk cross-section above the y=0 symmetry plane. Four named STL solids (inlet, outlet, walls, symmetry). Stresses two pipeline behaviors not yet covered by iter02..iter05: (a) curved cylindrical wall surfaces — gmsh splits the curved wall into multiple parametric surfaces, exercising per-triangle voting on quad strips of mixed orientation; (b) SYMMETRY BCClass driven end-to-end via named-patches mode — the class has been in bc_setup_from_stl_patches.py since cacda9f but no adversarial case has executed it.

symmetry.note: y=0 plane; OpenFOAM 'symmetry' boundary should mirror flow

mass_balance.expectation: volumetric_flow[inlet] ~= volumetric_flow[outlet] (symmetry plane contributes zero flux)

expected_outcome.qualitative_description: Steady-state pipe flow develops along +z. Velocity profile is parabolic at fully developed cross-sections (Poiseuille-like, but on half-disk). Axial Uz peaks near the cylinder axis (~0.5 m/s if undeveloped, up to ~1.0 m/s if fully developed in this ~6:1 length-to-diameter ratio). Symmetry plane should show no normal velocity component (zero v_y on y=0 face).

smoke_runner.rationale: Half-pipe with symmetry plane; validates SYMMETRY constraint type + curved walls.
